landing.heroDesc: Revolutionary innovation in particulate matter measurement technology — real-time quantitative data, AI-based qualitative analysis, and professionally valid assessment in one system
landing.problemsSubtitle: Protecting the air is one of the greatest tasks and challenges of environmental protection
landing.p1Desc: The density of the currently operating national monitoring network does not reach the desired level. Consequently, environmental engineering activities often use remote data, which is not always appropriate compared to local conditions.
landing.p2Desc: Currently available solutions are not suitable for detecting specific pollutants or hazardous substances — quantitative data alone does not provide a true picture of air quality.
landing.p3Desc: There is an urgent need to increase the number of measurement points and establish continuous, reliable monitoring in the vicinity of industrial facilities.
landing.howItWorksTitle: How does the analysis system work?
landing.howItWorksSubtitle: A complex network of measuring stations providing accurate, detailed, and easy-to-understand information about air quality
landing.s1Desc: A measuring station built from standard elements, suitable for large-scale deployment. The established network performs both quantitative detection and qualitative sampling.
landing.s2Desc: The Sensirion SEN50-SDN-T laser sensor integrated into the Sigma-2 passive dust sampler (VDI 2119:2013) provides continuous measurement and real-time data tracking via internet access.
landing.s3Desc: Detailed investigation of sample composition to detect hazardous substances through AI-based image analysis trained with electron and light microscopy images.
landing.s4Desc: Precise prediction of pollutant dispersion and future impacts is possible using the AERMOD air dispersion modeling software.
landing.techSubtitle: Standardized, certified elements in an innovative process
landing.t1Desc: Device standardized by the German Chamber of Engineers according to the VDI 2119:2013 standard
landing.t2Desc: Laser sensor — for measuring PM10, PM4, PM2.5, and PM1.0
landing.t3Desc: Chemical instrumental analytical control with artificial intelligence-based image analysis
landing.t4Desc: System developed by the United States Environmental Protection Agency

partners.title: Partners and Supporters
partners.subtitle: The project is realized with the collaboration of several renowned institutions
partners.p3Desc: Artificial intelligence and light microscopy imaging
partners.p4Title: Budapest University of Technology and Economics
partners.p4Desc: Chemical analysis and electron microscopy examinations
partners.p6Desc: Providing terrain for the pilot measurement network

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Find more content about us on the pages below
about.a1Desc: The project achieved second place. A brief description from that time can be found at www.otio.hu/157.html
about.a2Desc: A blog post by SungEel HiTech Hungary Ltd. about the revolutionary air protection monitoring stations and the installation of the pilot measurement network at the Bátorterenye site. https://www.sungeelht.hu/blog/levegominosegi-meresek-uj-technologia.html

InnOtdk competition. https://njszt.hu/hu/news/2025-11-25/beszamolo-az-ujit-innovacio-es-informatika-cimet-viselo-2025-evi-neumann

dev.title: Development Status
dev.desc: The project has reached level 6 on the Technology Readiness Level (TRL) scale, meaning the system prototype operates successfully under both laboratory and real-world conditions.
dev.trlDesc: The system prototype operates successfully under both laboratory and real-world conditions.

contact.title: Contact
contact.support: We are happy to assist you with any questions regarding the KLER system.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.p1Desc: KLER (Complex Air Quality Analysis System) is committed to protecting users' personal data. This policy outlines the data handled by the system.
privacy.p2Desc: During the use of our services, we record your provided email address, password (encrypted), name, and optional data needed for registration.
privacy.p3Desc: The purpose of data processing is managing user accounts, providing services, and improving user experience. The legal basis is the user's consent and the performance of the contract.
privacy.p4Desc: All provided data is stored on secure servers protected by modern encryption algorithms and is never shared with third parties without permission.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.p1Desc: Cookies are small text files stored in your browser by websites to ensure the website runs more efficiently and to improve user experience.
cookies.p2Desc: The system uses essential (session), analytical, and preference-saving cookies (e.g., language, theme).
cookies.p3Desc: We request your consent to use cookies upon your first visit (except for essential cookies required for system operation). You can disable or delete cookies in your browser settings at any time.

airQuality.title: Air Quality Analyzer
airQuality.subtitle: Real-time data from Budapest measuring stations
airQuality.chartSubtitle: Comparison of PM10, PM4, PM2.5, and PM1 values
airQuality.selectDevicePrompt: Please select a measuring device to display the details.
airQuality.aiPrompt:
You are a leading air quality expert. Your task is to compile a complex environmental health expert opinion based on the following data:

MEASUREMENT PARAMETERS:
Location: {{name}}
Time: {{timestamp}}
Particulate concentration: PM10: {{pm10}} µg/m3, PM4: {{pm4}} µg/m3, PM2.5: {{pm25}} µg/m3, PM1: {{pm1}} µg/m3
Particle composition: {{composition}}
Meteorology: {{temp}} °C, {{humidity}} % humidity, {{dewpoint}} °C dew point
Wind and precipitation: {{wind_speed}} km/h, wind direction: {{wind_dir}}, precipitation: {{rain}} mm

EXPECTATIONS FOR THE REPORT:

1. DATA SUMMARY: Start the report with a brief, factual listing of the measurement data.

2. AEROSOL ANALYSIS: Analyze the ratio of PM fractions. Explain what the distribution of fine (PM1, PM2.5) and coarser (PM10) particles indicates (e.g., combustion processes or mechanical dust generation). Evaluate the source of the specified composition.

3. METEOROLOGICAL CORRELATIONS: Explain how current wind speed affects ventilation, and how humidity or dew point influences particle settling or condensation.

4. HEALTH IMPACTS: Describe the physiological relevance of the measured levels, with particular regard to the risks of the smallest particles entering the lungs.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- STRICTLY NO Markdown formatting (do not use #, * characters or list signs).
- Divide the answer into coherent paragraphs with a professional but publicly understandable style.
- If any data is 0 or MISSING, indicate the uncertainty due to limited data, but do not guess.
airQuality.noData: No data available for the selected period.
